content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 24, 1994

Anthony Scarantino
Government Affairs
NationsCredit Corporation
1105 Hamilton Street
Allentown, PA 18101-8000

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-94-194

Dear Mr. Scarantino:

You have requested clarification of advice provided to
Richard Smith of NationsCredit Political Action Committee
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "PRA"). Your letter states only a general question.
Therefore, we consider it to be a request for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

Your letter correctly summarizes the advice provided to Mr.
Smith (Advice Letter No. I-93-404) that NationsCredit Political
Action Committee (the "Committee") will incur no filing
obligations in California if it makes contributions or
expenditures totaling less than $1,000 during a calendar year to
support or oppose California state or local candidates and ballot
measures.

However, whether contributions received by the Committee
from California residents are not relevant. Unless contributions
from California residents are specifically designated for use in
connection with California elections, all contributors to the
Committee would be deemed to have contributed a portion of any
expenditures made in California.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Anthony Scarantino
Our File No. T-94-194
Page 2


     I hope this information is helpful.  Please contact me at
(916) 322-5662 if you have more questions concerning this letter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Carla Wardlow
                                        Division Chief
                                        Technical Assistance Division

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the residency of contributors to a Political Action Committee affects filing obligations under California's Political Reform Act when the Committee makes contributions or expenditures in California.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Contributions received by the Committee are not relevant based on contributor residency. Unless contributions are specifically designated for use in California elections, all contributors would be deemed to have contributed a portion of any expenditures made in California. The Committee incurs no filing obligations if total contributions or expenditures in California total less than $1,000 during a calendar year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter (not formal opinion); no separate QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections with headers; question and answer synthesized from body text. Letter references prior Advice Letter No. 1-93-404. Recipient sought clarification of earlier advice provided to another party.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the residency of contributors to a Political Action Committee affects filing obligations under California's Political Reform Act when the Committee makes contributions or expenditures in California.

CONCLUSION: Contributions received by the Committee are not relevant based on contributor residency. Unless contributions are specifically designated for use in California elections, all contributors would be deemed to have contributed a portion of any expenditures made in California. The Committee incurs no filing obligations if total contributions or expenditures in California total less than $1,000 during a calendar year.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides informal assistance clarifying that a Political Action Committee's filing obligations in California depend on total contributions and expenditures, not contributor residency, and that contributions are attributable to all donors unless specifically designated otherwise.
